cases.notes: first byte announces a 4-byte varint62 but only 3 bytes are present
frame_length = 16387, code = DATA, stream_id = 4, derived payload length = 16385

cases.description: A preface settings block repeats the same setting ID.
A peer advertises an unsupported role value.
Both peers use role=auto with the same tie_breaker_nonce.
A peer uses role=auto with tie_breaker_nonce = 0.
A peer advertises max_frame_payload below the v1 minimum.
A peer advertises max_control_payload_bytes below the v1 minimum.
A peer advertises max_extension_payload_bytes below the v1 minimum.
A frame advertises frame_length smaller than the mandatory code plus shortest stream_id encoding.
EXT derives a payload shorter than the encoded ext_type prefix.
A frame_length remains too small for the actual encoded stream_id prefix after stream_id parsing.
PING carries a forbidden non-zero flag.
PONG payload is shorter than the required 8-byte token.
A locally originated PING chooses a payload longer than the smaller of local and peer control-payload limits.
ABORT is invalid on the session control stream_id 0.
MAX_DATA carries trailing bytes beyond its one canonical max_offset field.
BLOCKED carries trailing bytes beyond its one canonical blocked_at field.
A frame uses an unassigned core frame type.
PRIORITY_UPDATE repeats a singleton STREAM-METADATA-TLV.
PRIORITY_UPDATE contains a structurally truncated TLV header.
PRIORITY_UPDATE contains a TLV value length that overruns the enclosing payload.
PRIORITY_UPDATE arrives without negotiated priority_update capability.
PRIORITY_UPDATE targets a previously unused valid stream ID.
PRIORITY_UPDATE targets a stream that is already terminal.
An unknown EXT subtype is received.
A DATA frame uses OPEN_METADATA without negotiated open_metadata capability.
A DATA frame uses OPEN_METADATA after the stream is already open.
OPEN_METADATA repeats a singleton STREAM-METADATA-TLV on the opening DATA frame.
Incoming DATA would exceed the effective stream MAX_DATA limit.
A stream-scoped MAX_DATA arrives for a previously unused valid stream ID.
A stream-scoped BLOCKED arrives for a previously unused valid stream ID.
A STOP_SENDING arrives for a previously unused valid stream ID.
A RESET arrives for a previously unused valid stream ID.
Incoming DATA would exceed the effective session MAX_DATA limit.
A peer opens a new stream ID that skips lower still-unused IDs of the same class.
BLOCKED arrives from the non-sending side of a unidirectional stream.
MAX_DATA arrives from the sending side of a unidirectional stream.
STOP_SENDING arrives from the sending side of a unidirectional stream.
RESET arrives from the non-sending side of a unidirectional stream.
A later GOAWAY increases one of its per-kind acceptance watermarks.
A provisional local open is cancelled before first-frame commit but still advances the next local stream_id cursor.
Hidden control-opened-only nonterminal state exceeds the local hard cap but the implementation neither sheds nor refuses the newest hidden stream.
Many stopped directions together exceed the repository-default aggregate late-data allowance without triggering local shedding or termination policy.
A peer repeatedly opens and immediately aborts streams at abusive rates while the implementation applies no churn limit or disconnect policy.
